parsed.clarification_question: Should the response focus on scientific/technical validity and novelty, or on writing and presentation quality?

rationale.R1.1: The open-ended request invites assessment of the thesis, gap, and contribution framing of the proposed framework.
rationale.R1.2: A general opinion would reasonably include whether the internal argument from architecture and features to claimed generality is coherent.
rationale.R1.3: The reported performance and transfer claims make evidence-claim alignment a central responsibility for evaluating the artifact.
rationale.R1.4: The artifact positions CycleSenseNet against prior methods, so an opinion may centrally involve assessing novelty relative to those approaches.
rationale.RX.1: Any projection must disclose uncertainty because the delegation is underspecified and the artifact provides limited context.
rationale.RX.2: The projection must avoid inflating the requested responsibility beyond what the vague prompt supports.
rationale.RX.3: The projection must stay limited to responsibility assignment rather than answering or editing the artifact.
rationale.RX.4: The projection should account for potential downstream harm from unqualified technical or deployment-related judgments.
rationale.RX.5: The projection should trace responsibilities to specific features of the delegation and artifact.
